zh.Grave, North Brabant, Netherlands: 荷蘭努-布拉班特格拉福
zh.lifeSummary: 當Jan I van Cuijk於1230年出生在荷蘭努-布拉班特藍德 範 可格拉福時，他的父親Hendrik III van Cuijk35歲，母親Aleida Persijn van Putten46歲。他和Jutta von Nassau至少有6個兒子和6個女兒。他去世於1308年7月13日在荷蘭努-布拉班特可享年78歲，埋葬在荷蘭努-布拉班特格拉福。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Jan I van Cuijk于1230年出生在荷兰努-布拉班特藍德 範 可格拉福时，他的父亲Hendrik III van Cuijk35岁，母亲Aleida Persijn van Putten46岁。他和Jutta von Nassau至少有6个儿子和6个女儿。他去世于1308年7月13日在荷兰努-布拉班特可享年78岁，埋葬在荷兰努-布拉班特格拉福。

nameMeanings.name: van
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Anh, Thanh, Hung, Minh, Vinh, Dung, Hue, Huong, Lan, Quang, Tan, Thuy, Hong, Chi, Hang, Ho, Pok, Sang, Bai, Chin, Ching, Chu, Chuan, Dong.
Vietnamese (Vạn): from the Chinese surname 萬, see Wan 1.
Vietnamese (Văn): from the Chinese surnames 文 or 聞, see Wen 2 and 3, and also 5 below.

variants.name: Wan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Yi, Qi, Qiang, Wai, Yiu, Yuk, Chien-Min, Fang, Hin, Jichun, Li, Man, Chung, Min, Yiping, Pak, Sik, Sung Man.  Vietnamese Tin, Bang, Da, Hien, Hoi, Lan, Minh Quang, Sau, Tich, Tung, Yaping, Yuet.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 萬, which means ‘ten thousand’ literally or ‘very great number’ figuratively: (i) from the personal name of Bi Wan (畢萬), an official in the state of Jin (located in present-day Shanxi province). He was said to be a descendant of Bi Gong Gao (known as Duke of Bi), the 15th son of the virtuous King Wen of Zhou (reined 1152–1056  BC ). Bi Wan was later granted the fiefdom of Wei (魏), which in 403  BC  became the state of Wei (located in present-day Shanxi, Shaanxi, Henan and Hebei provinces). Some of his descendants adopted Wei (魏) as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his personal name, as their surname. (ii) from the personal name Wan (萬), style name of Rui Bo (芮伯, the Earl of Rui), an official of the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ), who was granted the fief Rui (located in Ruicheng in Shaanxi province). Some of his descendants inherited Rui (芮), the name of the fief, as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his style name, as their surname. (iii) from the placename Wan (萬), the name of a fief (located in Juxian in Shandong province) granted during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ) to an official in the ancient state of Chu. (iv) from the first element of the name of the Wan Si (萬俟) family, who lived in the downstream areas of the Yellow River during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ). (v) from the second element of the name of the Tu Wan (吐萬) family, who are members of the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 完, meaning ‘complete’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by the descendants of Huan Zhui, a grandson of the Duke Huan of Song (ruler of the state of Song, died 651  BC ). (ii) from Wan (完), the name of Qu Wan (屈完), an official in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn Period (770–476  BC ). (iii) shortened form of the compound surname Wan-Yan (完顔), a surname from minority ethnic groups such as Juchen ethnic group in ancient northern China.

nameMeanings.name: Jan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

en.lifeSummary: When Jan I van Cuijk was born in 1230, in Grave, Land van Cuijk, North Brabant, Netherlands, his father, Hendrik III van Cuijk, was 35 and his mother, Aleida Persijn van Putten, was 46. He had at least 6 sons and 6 daughters with Jutta von Nassau. He died on 13 July 1308, in Cuijk, North Brabant, Netherlands, at the age of 78, and was buried in Grave, North Brabant, Netherlands.

fr.Grave, Land van Cuijk, North Brabant, Ne: Grave, Land van Cuijk, Brabant-septentrional, Pays-Bas
fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Jan I van Cuijk est né en 1230, à Grave, Land van Cuijk, Brabant-septentrional, Pays-Bas, son père, Hendrik III van Cuijk, avait 35 ans et sa mère, Aleida Persijn van Putten, avait 46 ans. Il a eu au moins 6 fils et 6 filles avec Jutta von Nassau. Il est décédé le 13 juillet 1308, à Cuijk, Brabant-septentrional, Pays-Bas, à l’âge de 78 ans, et a été enterré à Grave, Brabant-septentrional, Pays-Bas.

de.Grave, Land van Cuijk, North Brabant, Ne: Grave, Land van Cuijk, Nord-Brabant, Niederlande
de.lifeSummary: Als Jan I van Cuijk 1230 in Grave, Land van Cuijk, Nord-Brabant, Niederlande geboren wurde, war sein Vater Hendrik III van Cuijk 35 und seine Mutter Aleida Persijn van Putten 46. Er hatte mit Jutta von Nassau mindestens 6 Söhne und 6 Töchter. Er starb am 13. Juli 1308 im Alter von 78 Jahren in Cuijk, Nord-Brabant, Niederlande und wurde in Grave, Nord-Brabant, Niederlande begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Jan I van Cuijk è nato il nel 1230 nel Grave, Land van Cuijk, Brabante settentrionale, Paesi Bassi, il suo padre, Hendrik III van Cuijk, aveva 35 anni e il suo madre, Aleida Persijn van Putten, aveva 46 anni. Lui avuto almeno 6 figli e 6 figlie con Jutta von Nassau. Morì il 13 luglio 1308, nel Cuijk, Brabante settentrionale, Paesi Bassi, all'età di 78 anni, e fu sepolto a Grave, Brabante settentrionale, Paesi Bassi.

ja.lifeSummary: Jan I van Cuijkが1230年にオランダノオルド-ブラバントランド ヴァン クウイジクグラブで生まれた時，父Hendrik III van Cuijkは35歳，母Aleida Persijn van Puttenは46歳であった。彼はJutta von Nassauとの間に少なくとも6人の息子と6人の娘をもうけた。彼は1308年7月13日にオランダノオルド-ブラバントクウイジクで78歳で死去し，オランダノオルド-ブラバントグラブにて埋葬された。

ko.Grave, Land van Cuijk, North Brabant, Ne: 네덜란드 노르드-브라반트 랜드 버 쿠이제이크 그레이브
ko.lifeSummary: Jan I van Cuijk(이)가 1230년 네덜란드 노르드-브라반트 랜드 버 쿠이제이크 그레이브에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Hendrik III van Cuijk(은)는 35세였고, 그의 어머니 Aleida Persijn van Putten(은)는 46세였다. 그는 Jutta von Nassau(와)과 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 여섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1308년 7월 13일 네덜란드 노르드-브라반트 쿠이제이크에서 78세의 나이로 사망했으며 네덜란드 노르드-브라반트 그레이브에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Jan I van Cuijk nasceu em 1230, em Grave, Land van Cuijk, Brabante do Norte, Países Baixos, seu pai, Hendrik III van Cuijk, tinha 35 anos e sua mãe, Aleida Persijn van Putten, tinha 46 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 6 filhos e 6 filhas com Jutta von Nassau. Ele faleceu em 13 de julho de 1308, em Cuijk, Brabante do Norte, Países Baixos, com 78 anos, e foi sepultado em Grave, Brabante do Norte, Países Baixos.

ru.Grave, Land van Cuijk, North Brabant, Ne: грав, ланд ван куиджк, нурд-брабант, Нидерланды
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Jan I van Cuijk родился в 1230 г. в местности грав, ланд ван куиджк, нурд-брабант, Нидерланды, его отцу по имени Hendrik III van Cuijk было 35 лет, а матери, которую звали Aleida Persijn van Putten, 46. У него было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 6 дочерей от Jutta von Nassau. Он умер 13 июля 1308 г. в местности куиджк, нурд-брабант, Нидерланды в возрасте 78 лет и был похоронен в местности грав, нурд-брабант, Нидерланды.
